methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $800.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 22 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpaceX is a Series G space and aerospace company that has raised over $10 billion to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $800 billion. The Space & Aerospace sector comprises 6 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs, though narrow peer-stage overlap remains limited.

Of three valuation methods examined, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier to SpaceX's $800 billion Series G post-money valuation, implying a forward value of $1.0 trillion. Peer-Median triangulation was not viable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Space & Aerospace universe (fewer than three datapoints meeting filter criteria). Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied because SpaceX, as a private company, does not disclose revenue; the median P/S multiple for public space comps is 49.8x, but without revenue data, this method yields no estimate.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $850 billion to $1.1 trillion, with a median point estimate of $1.0 trillion. The narrow methodological foundation—relying on a single applicable approach—represents the principal uncertainty. A more robust triangulation would require either disclosed private comparables at advanced stages or revenue disclosure enabling public-comp anchoring.
